a: Validate app and In-App Purchase receipts with the App Store.
Data types of objects that return in the receipt.
Verify the contents of app receipts by decoding and parsing the receipt on the device.
The UUID that an app optionally generates to map a customer’s in-app purchase with its resulting App Store transaction.
The renewal status for the auto-renewable subscription.
The time and date that the App Store refunds a transaction or revokes it from Family Sharing.
Error information that returns in the response body when a request isn’t successful.
The reason a subscription expires.
The time, in milliseconds, a subscription expires or renews.
The relationship of the user with the family-shared purchase to which they have access.
An indicator of whether an auto-renewable subscription is in the billing retry period.
An indicator of whether an auto-renewable subscription is in the introductory price period.
An indicator of whether an auto-renewable subscription is in the free trial period.
The offer-reference name of the subscription offer code that the customer redeems.
The transaction identifier of the original purchase.
The identifier of the promotional offer for an auto-renewable subscription that the user redeems.
The JSON contents you submit with the request to the App Store.
The JSON data that returns in the response from the App Store.
An array that contains all in-app purchase transactions.
An array of elements that refers to open or failed auto-renewable subscription renewals.
The decoded version of the encoded receipt data that you send with the request to the App Store.
An array that contains the in-app purchase receipt fields for all in-app purchase transactions.
The status of the app receipt.
A unique identifier for a transaction, such as a purchase, restore, or renewal.
Send a receipt to the App Store for verification.
